title: Quadlet does not seem to support templated volumes
body:
### Issue Description

Quadlet supports templated containers (`name@.container`), and it's often the case that each instantiated container would need its own separate volume storage, but Quadlet doesn't seem to have support for that. This heavily limits the usefulness of the templating feature.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Prepare the following two files:
```ini
# ~/.config/containers/systemd/foo@.container
[Container]
Image=quay.io/podman/hello
ContainerName=foo-%i
Volume=foo@%i.volume:/data:Z
```
```ini
# ~/.config/containers/systemd/foo@.volume
# empty file
```
2. Instantiate them by creating symlinks:
```bash
cd ~/.config/containers/systemd/
ln -s foo@.container foo@alpha.container
ln -s foo@.volume foo@alpha.volume
```
3. Run `/usr/lib/systemd/user-generators/podman-user-generator -dryrun`

### Describe the results you received

1. The generated volume template is `foo@-volume.service` rather than `foo-volume@.service`, and the generated volume instance is `foo@alpha-volume.service` rather than `foo-volume@alpha.service`
2. Error while generating template and instance for `foo@.container`:
```
quadlet-generator[14324]: converting "foo@.container": requested Quadlet source foo@%i.volume was not found
converting "foo@alpha.container": requested Quadlet source foo@%i.volume was not found
```

### Describe the results you expected

Quadlet should generate proper template and instance service files for `foo@.volume`, and it should be possible to reference them from `foo@.container`.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

ArchLinux x86_64. Everything is latest.

### Additional information

You can play around with trying to set a custom templated volume name in `~/.config/containers/systemd/foo@.volume`, but as far as my imagination has taken me, nothing worked.

comments.body:
I assume that is the same problem as described in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23215 (just for volumes instead of networks)
cc @ygalblum
@Luap99 yes it is. The issue is with resolving the connection between different templated Quadlet units of all types
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I believe #27169 has fixed this?

Although, it's worth noting that the way you're supposed to reference a templated `.volume`/`.network` in a templated `.container` is a little counter-intuitive IMO. I think it warrants at least a note in the manual. I'll create a PR for it.
